channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Food is so cheap it is practically free. — 94 replies — 376.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Food is so cheap it is practically free.

Open thread • Replies: 94 • Updated: 2026-03-03 10:40 UTC

OP

The image posted is in AUD, but for those in the US, Lets assume an hourly wage of $15.

$15 USD in the US can buy 25lbs of rice. which is 45,000 calories. that is astounding.

I work minimum wage (AUS) and after 2 hours of work I can afford an entire weeks worth of food.

the first hour affords me actually nearly a months worth of carbohydrate energy (rice, pasta and potato's)

The second hour affords me 3kg of meat. which is more than enough protein for a week.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Staple_food

very helpful link to understand there is actually not that much food in the entire world to eat for bulk calories/nutrition. there is about 7 different foods to eat. (wheat, rice, corn, potato, meat, dairy and bananas/plantains in certain regions, honorable mention for beans and lentils as well.)

Early replies (first 3)

61926835
People think they're too good for rice and beans, supplemented with fresh vegetables. They deserve to be poor. For the price of two colas you can buy an entire bag of beans which will feed you for weeks. Stop eating corposlop made in a lab that's marked up 10000%, eat real food.

61926840
>>61926822
>eating is so cheap when you eat like a 13th century

61926847
>>61926822
>food is so heckin cheap when you eat like a medieval Chinese peasant!
>updoots to the left!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61927363
>>61927352
No nutrients are missing with just the muscle meat alone. Fiber, oxalates, phytates, lectins and other plant toxins will just leech whatever nutrients you do eat. Worthless slop to fill the bellies of slaves without nourishing.

61927366
>>61926860
>you can just not pay for the fizzy corn syrup juice too.
Yea all that sounds nice but if I don’t get my fizzy corn syrup I start getting sick

61927371
>>61927352
vitamin K1, omega-3 fats (EPA/DHA), iodine, vitamin, and possibly magnesium. mainly from the lack of leafy greens, fatty fish, seafood, and a wider variety of fruits and vegetables. Mongoloid. Peasant. You are brown.

61927373
Vitamin D*

61927374
>>61927363
There are certainly nutrients missing in muscle meat.

Carbohydrate (a macronutrient) is an obvious one, Vitamin C is missing, Vitamin A is missing, Calcium, and there are plenty more.

61927382
>>61927371
Fatty fish and seafood, are meat. they are included. vitamin d is obtained from fatty fish as well as from exposing the skin to the sun.

also these oily fish are an excellent source of magnesium.

61927390
>>61927371
>k1
Red meat has superior k2
>o3
Red meat has plenty, most people eat way too much pufa anyway and they're terrible in excess
>iodine
Red meat has plenty, if it didnt the cow would die too
>magnesium
>leafy greens
Yeah eat the one food that actively strips magnesium out of your poop through oxalate binding, good shit
>fruits
Are completely destitute of nutrition and are mostly sugar
>vegetables
Dogshit defense chemicals, indigestible fiber, causes malabsorption
>fish
Can be ok but heavily poluted, farmed fish dogshit but still better than slavegrains and goyfeed rabbit food

Thank you for the financial advice. Seeing as the goyim are still this retarded, I will be going 100x long ond Pfizer tomorrow.

61927398
>>61927390
Still feeling homoerotic about pooping? You sound like a woman

61927400
>>61927374
>carbohydrate
Gluconeogensis, not required exogenously in any capacity
>vitamin C missing
Incorrect and retarded
>vitamin A is missing
Has the actual only real source of vitamin A (retinol). Plant based beta-carotene needs to be converted in the body, with many barely able to utilize even 5% of it based on their genes
>calcium
Plenty in muscle meat too. Plus excess calcium is also terrible for you.
The oxalates in leafy greens will actually strip calcium from your body.
You do realize if animals didnt have these things in them they'd be dead too right?

61927405
>>61927400
>Gluconeogensis
Yes, your body does everything it can to produce glucose, and carbohydrate is by far the easiest for the body to use for such purpose.
>Has the actual only real source of vitamin A (retinol).
Vitamin A is not stored in the muscles. it is stored in organs, which is not muscle meat.
>>61927400
>Plenty in muscle meat too.
It is very low in calcium.
You are better of just drinking a glass of milk.

61927417
>>61927405
>Yes, your body does everything it can to produce glucose
Anthropromorphising bodily functions and attributing "wants" to them is not an argument. There is still no requirement for exogenous carbohydrates for humans.
>>61927405
Vitamin A is stored in fat, not organs specifically. When you eat muscles you eat the associated fat, unless you're a freak.
>low in calcium
Plenty.

61927419
Another protip: Organ meat is typically a third of the price of the cheapest ground beef and has insane nutrition.

Even on a beans and rice poverty diet as long as you add a bit of liver and some fresh vegetables you will nail almost all of your micronutrients. Buy a simple manual meat grinder and you can cook organ meat into bolognese just like ground beef

I lived on 35 AUD a week for a year doing this and had zero health issues

61927442
>>61927417
Well to play semantics, when you say muscle meat, you are talking about the meat, the contractile tissue, fat is separate from this. as they are two different things, but sure. meat (muscle meat, but specifically liver and kidneys) definitely is necessary as it is the largest provider of nutrients by far.

61927498
>>61927022
You are a nigger and a jew.

61927603
>>61926835
Not to mention, if you're gonna waste your dopamine receptors, you might as well be creampie-ing hookers on ecstasy instead of eating cheeseburgers.

61927622
>>61926835

Based. Eating rice, beans and scrambled eggs is peak. Poorfags be cuck'd for the mc'ds tho. Wondering why they poor n tummy still upset. Sad.

61927677
>>61926822
Switched to keto 3 days ago.
I'm done with carbslop.
Rice and beans alone are mestizo feed and give you long term health issues.
Surprisingly it isn't costing me more than $5 a day, but I tend to buy things on discount.

61927697
food was absurdly cheap pre covid. 12 dollar lunch buffets. the 1 dollar mcchicken. sausage meats starting at 1 dollar per lb. that shit was so cheap i was buying them to feed to my raccoons.

now its more expensive but the basics are still pretty good. minimum wage can buy 2-4 dozen eggs per hour.

61927747
>>61926840
>>61926847
Rice with chicken and beans is infinitely more nutritious than Mcdonalds. Cry about it you are weak. Cheap food cooked well is the most beneficial.

61927938
>>61926835
>rice and beans, supplemented with fresh vegetables
this and tuna/deenz+salad is top tier cuisine
As a student I survived so well on this. Best food ever.

61928034
>>61927193
Soil has no very little to do with nutrients. Genetics of the plant has more control over nutrients, so the long lasting bland food that was bred to be that way is the problem. Having regional markets and actual seasons for strains of vegetables that taste good and don't have a long shelf life would contribute to having more nutrients in food.

61928068
>>61926848
Mountain Dew

61928075
>>61926822
>>61926835
Holy fuck. Irredeemably delusional.
Eating only rice and beans like one day you will wake up and have a meltdown about why you did that stupid overly frugal living shit to yourself. That is not gonna be a good day.

61928215
>>61926822
the chemicals and fake meat in Mcmuttslop are literal poison, at least in europe they force these evil fucks to make real food

61928231
>>61926822
Okay great, doesn't matter though when rent is your largest expense by a huge margin

61928338
>>61926867
fpbp

61928341
>>61926867
6 billion pajeets and gooks suggest otherwise

61928406
>>61926822
>calories

Amazing maths there. I better start drinking sunflower oil instead of eating food - that has twice the calories of rice! Just 11lbs of that already beats your 45000 calories bag of rice. Astounding

61928427
>>61926835
>vegetables
anything that isn't potatoes/carrot/cabbage/onion is expensive

61928442
>>61926822
>>61926835
>>61926870
Based. Most first world people just don't have any frugal skills. They fall for the consumer slop and blame there low wage for being broke.

item.title: Anyone else getting the itch? — 83 replies — 264.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Anyone else getting the itch?

Open thread • Replies: 83 • Updated: 2026-03-03 10:31 UTC

OP

The big boys'll go back up, that's guaranteed money. But some others will 10x, 100x, 1000x. I'm feelin' like it's SHIB's turn.

Early replies (first 3)

61900851
>>61899833
Checked

61900872
>>61899833
I'll just keep buying small amounts of it for the rest of my life, and see what happens.

61900948
>>61899833
i believe in shib

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61918170
Don't make last cycles mistake anon
Stay away from memecoins and bitcoin, 100% in ETH or XMR

61919529
Shib is fucking dead

61919535
>>61919529
yeah, kinda is
Thankfully i still got in early enough that there was never any chance of not making a profit despite missing out on the big gains through greed but there's nothing but a slow bleed right now and i've seen nothing that means it might pump again

61921116
Bro imagine buying that

61921137
Not today

61921143
No such thing as a free lunch

61921144
>>61899833
none of these shitcoins are gonna do big movements again retard

61921169
>>61921144
No such thing as a free lunch

61921558
Just bought some more boys, let's go

61921644
>>61901257
This already rugged.

61921961
>>61921558
Ummm based?

61921988
>>61921644
Checked and true

61922004
kek turd killer still being shilled here in 2026
lmfao nice AI pics btw

61922014
>>61922004

61922070
>>61922004
You have 2 more weeks wagie

61922078
I hope you don’t buy toad

61922734
No one is buying toad in a shib thread! Shib is going to 14x to ath and toad will go to zero. Shib already has an etf coming and toad has desperate curry flavored shills. LMAO!

61923250
>>61922734
When toad moons YOU'RE going to seethe, when toad moons YOU'RE going to rope!

61923260
>>61899833
I am in anon, people are too stupid to skip this one.
And some Doge in case the elom makes a comeback.

61924787
>>61922734
See
>>61922014

61926036
>>61899833
This thread smells like curry and shit.

Also, shibaggies holding their shibags to zero.

61926122
>>61926036
>This thread smells like curry and shit.
So like most threads on /biz ?

>>61926036
>Also, shibaggies holding their shibags to zero.
Sure buddy. The number 1 meme on eth is going to zero goy!

61927655
>>61926122
>The number 1 meme on eth is going to zero goy!
i'm genuinely considering dumping the rest of what i hold because of this post

61927803
>>61927655
Checked dump it

61927812
>>61899833
you will be lucky to get a 3x

61927816
>>61927812
Shib is ez 10x bear case 33x bull case

61927892
>>61927816
Never happening

61928058
>>61927892
>Shib will never go to a place it’s been before because it just won’t.
You sound tarded bro

61928094
>>61928058
It only went there because of insane hype and FOMO. It will never happen again unless Elon makes it the official currency of X or something

61928421
>>61927655
Please do fud chud. I like cheapies.
>>61927812
>>61928094
He didn't buy cheap before the etf and clarity act passed.
>NGMI!

item.content:encoded:
/pmg/ Pirates Metals Goblins

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-03-03 10:48 UTC

OP

ARR!

Early replies (first 3)

61926222
yarrr

61926235
>>61926222
arr har har harr! get ye telescope and check this shipmate!

61926254
Arrgh matey, IQCHADJEETs Silva stock bussin nigga frfr on kirk nigga

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61926270
>>61926208
YOO HOO HOO BITCESS!

61926281
>>61926208
BOY I SURE LOVE SI

61926339
>>61926208
for real though HEAR ME, LAND LUBBERS!
THIS BE YE FINAL CALL TO BOARD!
Watch the charts, and plot your course carefully! The seas be stormy, but hold fast, and ye'll make port with a booty greater than any other in this time! It may be many moons but ye'll come up smiling yet!
Watch for the crests of the waves and the troughs, and cast ye line into those dips! However, take heed, this will be your last entry.
The last cup is forming.
Be sure to fill it to the brim (with rum) and slurp deeply!
Lest ye regret.

61926355
jane street = jail jeets

61926390
How the fuck is this price action depressing you faggots

61926425
>>61926390
? it's one of the few things lifting my spirits.
i expect a little dip, but that only means better plunder!

61926436
>>61926425
Yeah I will probably buy something
Maybe halves

61926443
>>61926436
Forgot pic

61926484
where are the goblin girls

61926555
>>61926484
there have been ... advancements.

61926651
>>61926208

This is your friendly reminder that precious metals are for faggots and foids.

61926684
>>61926484
best i can do is a wraparound spider

61926808
>>61926651
no they are only for pirates and goblins. we plundered them.

61926820
Is this enough to make it?

61926827
>>61926820
hmm that'll buy a very small ship, but a ship it will be.

61926903
>>61926808

The only thing you plundered is some dude ass.

61926982
>>61926903
nay, it were gold and silver from a box! i speak ye true, plundered some gold and silver recently, found a discarded jewelery box in some trash, had some silver chains and a gold bracelet in it! not massive, but some real loot! tripple digits in value.

61926996
>>61926982
Did Mr. T get into a fight with his wife? If so that is quite a treasure there.

61927254
Why have silver naysayers been so quiet lately?

61928405
>>61926820
nope. junk silver is worthless and you have barely any. Is that actual gold or just random coins?

61928457
Judas was able to use junk silver to buy a huge field of land. What was true back then will be true again. The world will come full circle.

item.title: Does IQ make you more successful financially? — 20 replies — 80.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Does IQ make you more successful financially?

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-03-03 10:46 UTC

OP

Does IQ make you more successful financially?

Early replies (first 3)

61927833
>>61927814
No, but emotional intelligence does.

61927872
>>61927833
>emphaty
retard detected

61927878
>>61927814
>>61927833
>>61927872


now post her without make up and filters and 0.1sec clips

i'll wait

fucking yellow fever fags

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61927881
>>61927833
>emotional intelligence
participation trophy

61927886
>>61927833
Most of those things are barely related to either emotions or intelligence

61927916
>>61927814
I'm pretty confident I wouldn't be a millionaire now if I didn't have a strong background in math. That strong background generally requires a higher than average IQ.

61927931
FOMO is an anxiety. If you can't reflect and regulate your own emotions you will lose money.
https://youtu.be/bEXz7Abf-wM?si=5Fm5SX09qPE0ycgq

61928032
The four cardinal virtues are also a helpful concept. Achieving them is a sign of maturity.
-fortitude
-justice
-temperance
-prudence

61928033
>>61927814
No, self-control and practical mathematics applied to real-world problems and risk management are what make you successful relative to your peers.

You can be high-IQ and have a very strong grip on abstract mathematics, while having zero emotional control or risk management.

Personality is more of a predictor of your success than IQ. But, a higher IQ can equal a higher income, but not more wealth, because you may blow your income on consumable slop, or lose it for whatever reason.

I'm 32 years old and work a basic factory wagie job in the rural Midwest. I'm also in the top 7% of networth for people my age, because I'm extremely good with finance and have the right personality for it.

OCEAN Test Results:

O = High
C = High
E = Middle
A = Low
N = High

61928043
>>61928033
this

61928081
>>61927814
God I need a plastic chinkgook like this to step on me

61928334
>>61927833
>Motivation
What does that have to do with emotional intelligence? Even retards who can never read the room can have great motivation.

61928351
>>61928081
watch out. she will spank you with her spring onions, anon.

61928387
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Emotional_intelligence

61928394
What if somebody wants to learn the latin language and shit. Maybe they font want to optimize the tiktok.

61928410
>>61927814
Yes, everything else is cope and lies of low IQ people who really want to be high IQ and tells that kind of lies to them selfs.

61928414
Asian pussy makes you successful. That's why WMAF is the future.

61928426
>>61927833
>Motivation
All is vanity as the preacher said. I can do things though but my heart will not be in any of them
>Social skills
Yes, i have them but i hate people and choose to avoid them like the plague
>Empathy
Real intelligence entails high levels of empathy. I get you're sad but i won't cry with you because your cat has died. I don't expect you to cry if my father dies. I won't. It's the circle of life, Simba.
>Self awareness
I can read a room perfectly enough to know when my antisemitic jokes haven't landed as i expected
>Self regulation
Buzzword i don't know what it means. I can regulate and adjust myself according to the situation.

61928448
>>61927814
I have ~130IQ and i'm a poorfag NEET college dropout at 24. I have very little self-control or motivation so it's hard to get and hold a job, and I am very socially awkward so it's hard to make connections (the only way to get any skilled job nowadays), so i ended up an outcast. Many people in that range seem to have similar issues.

However, if you have 110-120IQ you probably will be "normal" enough to not be a weird outcast and smart enough to get ahead of most normalfags, and if you're >145IQ you're a genius and will probably end up being world-class at something.

I'm not trying to sadpost or get sympathy, it's just what I've observed.

61928455
>>61928448
Yea being king of the midwits is the best, you just crash out if you have le intelligence and realize we live in hell

item.title: STRC now pays 11.5% dividend. — 18 replies — 46.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
STRC now pays 11.5% dividend.

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-03-03 10:27 UTC

OP

Is there anyone who thinks that this shit is going to end well?

Early replies (first 3)

61924371
>he isn't looping STRC
ngmi

61924448
>>61924371
Holy shit is finally the end of Bitcoin...

61924465
>>61924170
Strategy's debt is structured quite defensively. Almost all social media commentary completely misses the point because those lazy asses don't even look into how the company an its products work.
STRC in particular pays an adaptable, voluntary dividend. If this product is going to become more popular it's automatically going much lower, maybe more like 5%, but given its voluntary nature and Strategy's opportunism, even if it isn't received well they could also do something like reduce to 3% or 0% and buy back shares to keep the $100 peg instead of depending on the dividend.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61924471
>>61924170
it's a scam and ponzi scheme don't buy it (I just bought more in my Roth today)

61924481
>>61924465
Is a turbo ponzi.

61926067
>>61924170
Saylor is going to have 1m+ BTC by the time all the banks start begging customers to custody with them.

Saylor is going to be the richest guy in the world.
He's never selling, only buying, forever

61926172
or i could just buy btc and btci

61926212
Do you hear that? That's the sound of future wholecoiners being vaporized

61926292
>>61924371
unironically LUNA 2.0 but worse

61926299
>>61924465
>buyback the shares
if you're suggesting they sell btc to do this you know this is how a deathspiral happens

61926327
>>61924170
good thread, what's the options play here

61926359
>>61924170
The big takeaway should be about how long banks have been fucking people with low interest rates on deposits

61926596
>>61926359
Anybody with two brain cells can finda a HYSA or buy a bond ETF

61928120
>>61924170
Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) keeps the STRC (Stretch) Variable Rate Series A Perpetual Preferred Stock distribution around 10% (ranging between 9% and 11.5% as of early 2026) primarily by utilizing a variable, monthly adjusted dividend rate designed to keep the security trading close to its $100 par value.
The company employs several active management mechanisms to maintain this stability and yield:
Monthly Dividend Adjustment: The Board of Directors adjusts the dividend rate monthly based on the five-day volume-weighted average price (VWAP) of the stock. If the stock price dips below $100 (e.g., during high volatility in Bitcoin), the company increases the dividend to make it more attractive to buyers and pull the price back to par.
At-the-Market (ATM) Offerings: Strategy uses an ATM program to issue new STRC shares, generally targeting a narrow band of $99–$101. This allows them to manage liquidity and support the price, with a $4.2 billion ATM program in place as of late 2025.
Bitcoin-Backed Overcollateralization: Each share of STRC is backed by Bitcoin at a ratio of roughly 5-to-1 (roughly $5 of BTC for every $1 of annual dividend). This immense collateral provides a cushion that reassures investors of the safety of the dividend, allowing for a lower required yield to maintain investor confidence.
Redemption/Call Rights: Strategy has the option to redeem (call) STRC shares at a set price (e.g., $101 per share plus accrued dividends) if the price rises significantly above par, preventing it from trading too high, or as part of active portfolio management.
Cash Buffer: To ensure consistent, high-yield payouts without needing to sell their Bitcoin treasury during down markets, Strategy has established a cash buffer, such as the $1.44 billion reserve announced in late 2025, specifically to fund preferred dividends

61928155
This shit has never worked, ever. It will crash and burn.

61928309
>>61926299
You might be a nice guy, but you're precisely the typical lazy ass commentator I was referring to.
In addition to
>selling bitcoin
they
>already have a cash reserve covering 2y worth of obligations
>can top it up by diluting common stock
>can top it up by selling more STR products
>can top it up by putting out more convertible notes, etc
So if you had ever looked in what they do you'd they can stomach another 1-2 years of bear market without doing anything at all, and then they have 3 fallbacks to generate more cash, and finally they can also sell bitcoin, which due to the cylical nature of this asset may not be a terrible idea either. Sometimes you sell, sometimes you buy, MSTR tries never to sell, but they are extremely opportunistic and can sell at an opportune time if is necessary to do this.

61928376
>>61928309
The fear around them selling as something catastrophic is exaggerated. If you boast of of being over collateralized, selling the collateral has to be an option on the table and as you pointed out they have several options before they'd have to sell to cover dividends.

61928415
>>61928376
Yes, and even if they do sell, they can do a single transaction to cover a year or so of obligations.
This might move the market -2% or so in the process, then -9% when they announce what they did and the inevitable FUD around MSTR selling takes hold, but the market will then have another year or so to calm down and forget about it until the next sale becomes necessary.
The MtGox and Germany government BTC dumps didn't make a sustained dent either, and those were higher amounts and weeks of FUD.

item.title: we are the cuckold marines — 9 replies — 20.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
we are the cuckold marines

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-03-03 10:24 UTC

61925155

Fucking kek, at least one of these cults got the memo and dumped it all.

61925403

61928411
>>61923201
Trust the plan my fellow cuckolds

item.title: AI creates new jobs they say... maybe in the energy and mineral extraction sector — 4 replies — 6.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
AI creates new jobs they say... maybe in the energy and mineral extraction sector

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-03-03 10:15 UTC

OP

>AGORA (AGOP.WA) Polish media group in December said it planned to lay off up to 166 employees, or 6.56% of its workforce, as part of a restructuring to improve its digital business.
>ALLIANZ (ALVG.DE) The German insurance group plans to cut up to 1,800 jobs in its travel insurance division as AI increasingly replaces manual processes, a source familiar with the plans told Reuters last November.
>AMAZON (AMZN.O) The tech giant confirmed 16,000 corporate job cuts on Jan. 28, leaving open the possibility of further reductions as it pursues an AI‑ and efficiency‑driven overhaul.
>AUTODESK (ADSK.O) The U.S. design‑software maker said on Jan. 22 it would shed about 7% of its global workforce, or roughly 1,000 jobs, as it redirects spending to its cloud platform and AI initiatives.
>BRITISH AMERICAN TOBACCO (BATS.L) The cigarettes, tobacco and nicotine products seller announced on February 12 a new AI-driven productivity programme expected to lead to job cuts, without specifying how much of the workforce would be affected.
>DOW (DOW.N) The U.S. chemical producer said on January 29 it will slash about 4,500 jobs, 13% of its total workforce, as it streamlines all its end-to-end work processes by using automation and AI.
>HP INC (HPQ.N) The U.S. computer and printer manufacturer said last November it expects to cut 4,000 to 6,000 jobs globally by fiscal 2028 as it streamlines operations and adopts AI.
>MERCADOLIBRE (MELI.O) The Brazilian e‑commerce company laid off 119 people in an AI‑expansion move, Folha de S. Paulo reported on Jan. 12.
>META (META.O) The owner of Facebook and Instagram is cutting over 1,000 jobs at its Reality Labs unit in a pivot from the Metaverse to AI devices, Bloomberg reported on Jan. 13.
>Meta is also cutting around 600 positions out of the several thousand roles in its Superintelligence Labs, the social media platforms owner said in October.

Early replies (first 3)

61926414
>>61926408
>NIKE (NKE.N) The sportswear giant is laying off 775 employees, a source familiar with the matter told Reuters in January, as the firm looks to boost profits and accelerate its use of automation.
>PINTEREST (PINS.N) The social‑media platform said in January it will cut up to 15% of its workforce to redirect resources toward AI‑focused roles and strategy.
>SEB (SEBF.PA) The French small appliance and cookware maker said on February 25 it would launch a restructuring plan that also takes "full advantage" of the possibilities offered by AI that may impact up to 2,100 jobs worldwide by 2027.
>TELSTRA (TLS.AX) Australia’s biggest telecoms firm plans to cut 650 jobs in an AI‑driven restructure with India’s Infosys (INFY.NS) The Australian reported on Feb. 11.
>WISETECH (WTC.AX) The Australian software company said on Feb. 25 it will axe about 2,000 jobs, nearly one‑third of its global workforce, as it integrates AI into customer software and internal operations.

61926575
>>61926408
This will be on your tombstone: "He believed jewish lies"

61928390
Goldman Sachs economists have projected a rise in unemployment this year from 4.3% to 4.5%, noting upside risks from faster adoption and larger displacement.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61928393
Sorry sir, we have a strict $100 dollar per day withdrawal limit.

item.title: Oil price — 55 replies — 64.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Oil price

Open thread • Replies: 55 • Updated: 2026-03-03 09:58 UTC

OP

>Street of Hormuz de-facto closed for at 24h
>20-25% of global oil supply cut from market
>Refineries attacked and closed in Saudi Arabia
>Tankers attacked on/near Street of Hormuz
>Tanker insurance fees through the roof
>Regime wants no negotiations with US
>Army and Guards still loyal to Regime
>Bloomberg predicts prices around 100$/barrel Brent

Still Oil price is going down. Merely up 5% from Friday. What the fuck is going on?!

Early replies (first 3)

61925169
Priced in

61925191
>>61925117
>>Regime wants no negotiations with US
Nice try moshi

61925221
>>61925169
How can an indefinite cut of 20-25% of global oil supply be priced in with like 16$ increase since start of 2026?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61925605
>>61925417
>I think the very real risks are being downplayed
Agreed. "Nothing ever happens" has become a crowded trade.

61925615
>>61925482
>kinda late for that
What do you mean late, it's been happening since day 1 of this war. Iran bombed a ton of NATO bases, you think China isn't interested in that? The only question is whether or not China is fully committed to this. They could be bombing those bases for years, forcing NATO to move more and more weapons to the middle of the desert so that they can shoot a 10k drone with a 10mil missile.
And China did supply Iran with a bunch of stuff between these two wars, let's see if they continue, they're trying to be sneaky. Not sure why though, EU isn't sneaky about giving weapons to Ukraine.
>>61925522
>he thinks US decides when it ends
If US doesn't do a land invasion, Iran can just keep bombing those NATO bases forever. Why stop

61925652
>>61925615
Well you had the supreme leader sitting in his house instead of hiding in a bunker when he had about a week's notice that the US was coming to fuck his shit right up. Now you have a situation where they're scrambling to rebuild their leadership. So how is Iran going to be a good proxy without leadership and organization?

61925667
>>61925117
>oil is only up 5%
scroll out retard oil is going up since december

61925671
>>61925117
priced in

61925673
>>61925300
He's talking about the oil that passes the Strait of Hormuz, not the current price action tard.

61925704
>>61925673
I'm fully aware of what he is talking about.
Read his post again:
>Still Oil price is going down. Merely up 5% from Friday. What the fuck is going on?!

61925726
>>61925704
You're right

61925729
>>61925481
BBC News is now reporting that ships have been attacked in the Strait of Hormuz. Cat is out of the bag now.

61925761
>>61925117
Wall street fat cats agreed to help dump while trump bombs iran.
Mostly because they know if Oil gets a green dildo then you can kiss your low CPI scores goodbye. Which then means wall street can kiss any hope of low rates goodbye.

That’s what this is ultimately about, FED rates.

61925906
should i go all into oil lads? seems like it should be pumping, but its not...also crypto is going up when it shoudln't be..clown world

61926020
>>61925906
It's not yet. That's the key. It's a bet on if this is going to keep going.

61926068
No more bullshit on CNBC. Iran says the strait is closed due to aids and any ship that passes through will be set on fire.

61926102
>>61926068
HOLY FUUUCCKIN SHIIT MY LONGS ARE POOOOOOOOOMPING

61926691
>>61925117
Price increases would cover insurance premiums going up. If the premiums go up but the price doesn't go up transporting oil becomes unprofitable. This could exacerbate any shortages in the long term.

61926787
>>61925117
>>61925169
>>61925191

Literally nothing is priced in. VIX is 21 and oil barely closed at 72. Market thinks this is a nothingburger.

If you disagree buy 10k worth of VIX or USO calls April expiry and take the 7x. That’s all there is to it. Shits not in the price.

61926796
>>61926691
https://infra.economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/ports-shipping/war-risk-premiums-surge-as-hormuz-crisis-forces-rerouted-sailings-threatening-indias-trade/128933982?dt=2026-03-02

61926861
>>61925117
Based. Total westoid humiliation.

61926893
Let's make this perfectly clear.

https://k.sina.cn/article_1887344341_707e96d504001qa2g.html
Translation:
>According to vessel tracking platform TankerTrackers.com, more than 50 tankers remain in Iranian waters, 18 of which are loaded with crude oil and 37 are empty.

>Premiums are soaring.

But as said in the link here: >>61926796

>On Sunday, GIC Re issued notice of cancellation for its marine hull war risk cover, stating that the cover will cease from March 3. The insurer has drawn up a high-risk area that includes Pakistan waters, Persian or Arabian Gulf and adjacent waters/ports including the Gulf of Oman west of Longitude 58 degree east, Iran and all other countries under sanction by the UN, the UK, the US or the EU, Sea of Azov and Black Sea waters, waters of Russia, Ukraine/ Belarus, India Ocean, Gulf of Aden, and Southern Red Sea.

>“In case any vessel passes through this area or calls any port located in this area or dry docked in any of these areas, it will be a breach of warranty,” GIC Re wrote in a circular issued on Sunday, adding that the breach of warranty cover will not be available in respect of all the seven zones mentioned in the high-risk area.

So if I get this right it didn't even matter that much that Iran actually attacked ships. The threat they made to do so going forward made it impossible to get insurance to pass through. So the strait is effectively closed not just by way of Iran's actions but because of the insurers. The market's optimism today was delusional. This news will spread.

61926901
>>61925534
It’s just a few airstrikes dude, it’s not that deep.

61926908
>>61926901
It won't take long for you to realize the economic damage done.

61926914
>>61926908
To them yeah, not over here. I’m just going to keep profiting from my oil tanker stocks in the meantime.

61926920
>>61926914
lol

lmao even

61926942
>>61925117
token not needed

61927578
>>61926893
Based.

61927593
>>61925522
Both Hegseth and Trump haven't ruled out boots on the ground

61927775
>>61926893
Yup, and like I said with the price increase covering the cost of insurance, if the price doesn't go up to compensate to the insurance even when the insurance is being issued, it won't be economically justifiable to take out a journey which purchases that insurance. A low price of oil will close the straight down until the price increases enough to justify running the blockade.

An unknown risk factor is also a refusal by crews to take those routes. It is unclear if there is any kind of danger pay, but I would imagine that workers might start demanding it tankers keep getting hit.

The price should be shooting up by a lot, but we psychologically in an end phase cycle of a bullmarket where people don't think bad things can happen. Rather than price responding to condition, conditions respond to price, so people will just act like "oh well I guess if the price didn't go up by that much it isn't a big deal" as the usefulness of a measuring stick (like GDP) is inversely correlated to how much focus actors place on it. If people are watching the oil price as a signal to determine how bad the war is going then the American government has reason to try to suppress the price for the same reason that it suppresses casualty figures. The price of oil right now is part of the propaganda. It is definitely going to wreak havoc eventually, but it isn't reacting because the reaction being known would negatively impact US foreign policy.

Instead of the price responding to the conditions, the conditions will respond to the price and keep the straight closed in practice even without Iran doing anything

61927855
>>61925117
>What the fuck is going on?!
World is in a economic recession.

61928364
So it seems the market is slowly waking up

item.title: So due to American involvement in Venezuela and Iran, America now majority owner of oil and China majority owner of mine… — 13 replies — 24.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
So due to American involvement in Venezuela and Iran, America now majority owner of oil and China majority owner of mine…

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-03-03 10:18 UTC

OP

So due to American involvement in Venezuela and Iran, America now majority owner of oil and China majority owner of minerals.

Honestly, from a strategic point this was a good move Trump made, but unethical.
Without this America wouldve lost more global influence by the year. Now they can somewhat prolong total defeat. China just needs to continue progressing as usual for them to defeat America, but America needs to change global policy quick to enhance GDP and be more involved with global trade.

Early replies (first 3)

61927971
>>61927909
Mmm sex! Didnt read.

61928026
nn

61928046
>>61927971
this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61928051
>>61927909
>China majority owner of minerals.
For now*

61928071
>>61927909
why is there a butt crease on the front?
is this AI?

61928110
>>61928071
my mommy did this in front of me, girls have two butts. a mud butt and a blood butt. she let me smell it. haha

61928172
>>61927909
I'd rather talk about the Epstein files and mossad control over America and the financial elite

61928204
>>61928172
Looking at how Russia is abandoning Iran, it's obvious they are compromised by Israel also.
Are Chinese the still free?

61928214
>>61927909
>"If the conflict was long-lasting and disrupted oil supplies, we expect the U.S. dollar would lift against most currencies except Japanese yen and Swiss franc. The U.S. is a net energy exporter and so benefits from higher oil and gas prices that would result from disrupted oil supply."

61928245
>>61927909
brap

61928249
Did they take out iran?

61928357
>>61928204
yeah it's weird how Russia has abandoned all their allies
First Syria, then Venezuela and now Iran.

61928403
>>61928110
He no longer pontificates the aroma.

item.title: iExec RLC — 19 replies — 18.1/hr

iExec RLC

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-03-03 09:47 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61920216
>>61919880
just wait 2 years and it will 4x

61920259
>>61919880
Soon

61920494
I can’t believe i am still holding this…

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61920746
>>61920494
please tell me you didn't buy at $2 you were supposed to take profits anon...you should be accumulating now and selling at $1.20

61920840
>>61919880
RETURN OF THE OILERS
NELLY FOR WORLD PRESIDENT
RLC TO $100, LFG!!!

61920930
Sold a while back, but then I bought this when I had no money so who cares.

61921104
Why is it dropping in value over this 6 months? Is there some secret bearish news about it? I used to be in it before the coinbase dump but sold for living expenses and stuff. Thinking of getting back into it but dont want to catch a falling knife

61921208
>>61921104
Idk anon. This was the first shit coin I ever believed in and held for multi years. I sold like 3 months before the Coinbase listing after buying during the mania phase of the 2017 bull run. Needless to say I lost money and I have a very strong hate for this coin and everyone who shills it. Having said all this, they are getting in the privacy scene with TEE and shit. Could be another grift. Also I want to make sweet love to Nelly for hours.

61921224
>>61919880
>digital oil

61921260
>>61920494
haha I can't believe I doubled downed and boughted mored
>>61921208
She is quite attractive.
Question is, all this TEE shit, it needs to ACTUALLY LAUNCH and be used.. real demand

61921277
Haha I forgot about this shit. I got like 15 tokens in some wallet somewhere. Hopefully you pump to that $8700 value or whatever so I can pay off my truck

61922291
>>61921277
checked. I'm skeptical. wish I was not....

61922301
L M F A O
L O L
T O P F U C K I N G K E K!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

61922385
>>61921277
>>61921208
This coin has been over $3 multiple times for months at a time, bots love this coin don't believe the FUD i know their website's a joke but this is clown world everything is fake none of these coins have any utility other than monero

61926014
TO THE MOON

61926018
I hate this piece of shit with a passion both for the 6 months I held it in 2020-2021 and the fact I sold it right before it did a 20x.

61926024
>>61922385
zcash>monero

61926048
>>61919880

61928348

item.title: >AIIIIIEEEEEEE DON'T SELL — 48 replies — 17.6/hr

OP

61924202

61924210

61924222

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61924447

61924452

61924459

61924479

61924494

61924558

61924600

Bless DJT

61924637

61924674
>>61924479

61924691
>>61924190

61924712

61924715

61924755

61924762

61924893

61924904

but then he is legitimately a senile moron who never read a book soooo... idk.

61926608

STOP SELLING
YOU WILL FUCK THE ECONOMY REEEEE

61926626
>>61924270

61927505
>>61924479

61928146

61928154
>>61928151

item.title: What do you even spend your money on besides basic necessities? — 25 replies — 17.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
What do you even spend your money on besides basic necessities?

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-03-03 09:24 UTC

OP

What do you even spend your money on besides basic necessities? Meaning YOUR OWN money, not from your parents or partner

Early replies (first 3)

61927797
>>61927791
I spend it to make more money lolls

61927810
>>61927797
Besides that, obviously.

61927824
>>61927791
junk silver coins
vintage synthesizers
railroad chronometers
vintage casio watches (next rolex)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61927826
>>61927791
Besides investments,
Guns(can still be investment), movies and vidya(entertainment), Better equipment for recording audio and video as I’m planning on becoming a boudoir and wildlife photographer this year.

61927834
>>61927824
What do you do with these? Just collect or resell them? I don't understand people that spend their money just to collect shit

61927849
>>61927834
They're toys. I wear those watches, fondle those coins, make sounds with those synthesizers. I don't plan to sell them while I'm alive, and when I die it will be someone else's headache.

61927864
>>61927826
Do you plan to make money from photography or just for your own pleasure? How is photography a pleasure?

61927890
>>61927849
Hello my cat. If I dangle some keys in front of you and tickle your belly will you pay me?

61927907
pc gaming but I hold myself to one full build every 5 years.

61927919
>>61927864
My hope is to make money from photography eventually, though I know it will take a while to turn a profit as a new photographer with no network after spending all the money I have on equipment. But I would also like to do it for fun as well. For boudoir, I’d get to meet many attractive models and even though sex won’t be involved it would still be nice to see them in person and get money from working with them. For wildlife, I have noticed in the past few years after being a shut-in most of my life that I crave adventure in the outdoors and I want to explore the wilderness in many different states and countries and hopefully get good enough photographs to be bought, and even if they aren’t I can keep them for myself. I’m not expecting to become rich from any of it but I’m hoping it can lead to some decent side money as well as a fulfilling hobby.

61927933
>What do you even spend your money on besides basic necessities?
I don't

61927945
>>61927919
What made you want to get into photography? Do you think AI will be fucking this shit up?

61927955
>>61927791
>video games
>musical instruments (new ocarina latest)
>books, mostly philosophy - reading Wittgenstein atm
>nicotine patches because I watched a single interview from a random doctor I've never heard of before or since who said its good for you
I also want to buy a Nikon P1000 or similar because space photography would be cool
>https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=G2j2WYYd3to

61927998
>>61927955
>>nicotine patches because I watched a single interview from a random doctor I've never heard of before or since who said its good for you
How do I become like you?

61928103
>>61927945
I’ve watched a lot of documentaries, especially ones about nature and war. I don’t think I would survive being a war photographer but I still like being out in nature a lot.
I guess I just want to know what it’s like to go out into various wilderness areas and capture the life cycles of various animals while taking in the scenery as well. I think I would enjoy it very much and even if no one buys my photos or videos I can always keep them and have the pleasure of the experience as well as having high quality wildlife photography of my own making. I already know I’d like to travel to far off places from me like Alaska or Japan to find certain bugs and animals.

As for the boudoir photography, I am simply a gooner that would get to work with some of the same girls I would beat it to, which sounds nice.

I don’t know about the future of AI, but for now I don’t think they could compete with real photographers when it comes to mainstream audiences. As for photography enthusiasts, I don’t think they will care about or accept AI in their work ever since the craft is about going to a real place and taking real pictures of moments of life.

61928112
>>61928103
Sounds like you're not that materialistic and you enjoy nature a lot. You realized money is a means, not an end. Good for you mang

61928131
>>61927791
Hookers

61928202
>>61927791
A big chunk of my non-essential spending has always gone towards international travel. When I was still working, I mostly spent money on DCA into investments. Now most of my discretionary spending goes towards visiting new places every few months.

61928211
>>61928202
How do you spend your money while traveling?

61928226
>>61928211
It depends on where I went and if I was single. Of course food, airplane, and mid-range hotels anywhere I go. Sometimes regular tourist shit like museums, boat tours, or hot air balloons. Other times bars, clubs, and women.

There was a 4-5 year phase I went through with music festivals within USA. That cost quite a bit of money between airplane tickets, car rentals, venue tickets, drugs, etc.

61928229
>>61928226
So I'm guessing ultimately the biggest expenses still revolve around getting laid?

61928256
>>61928229
It's the airfare and hotels that will get ya. Drinks and ladies are cheap in comparison even if that's wait you're mainly looking for.

61928263
>>61928112
Ty fren. I try to not be too jewish about money these days but at the same time I can’t stop thinking about my investments and money in the back of my head every day. I hope to become a successful photographer one day to alleviate these worries for myself and focus on something I’m passionate about.

61928282
>>61928263
I can see you already have it in you friend. If you are passionate you will find a way. And the "means" will come to you if you want it hard enough. You never know how or when, maybe you'll make a lot of money or maybe there's a different way for you. Just be open and present when the opportunities present themselves

61928307
>>61927791
I buy video games, beer, and that's it.

item.title: Buy alts now. Anything with fully diluted supply and reasonable history will suffice. It's quite clear at this point alt… — 23 replies — 15.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Buy alts now. Anything with fully diluted supply and reasonable history will suffice. It's quite clear at this point alt…

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-03-03 09:23 UTC

OP

Buy alts now. Anything with fully diluted supply and reasonable history will suffice.

It's quite clear at this point alts are doing an XRP of last run. Most have had their relatively shallow ~85% drawdown at this point and will continue to range from here with small pumps inbetween. BTC might drop to finish it's bottom later this year but alts will likely pump before that happens to respect the Feb26 bottom. You're looking at a mild spring/summer pump. The real pump won't be until late 2027/early 2028 (frontrun again, yes). Alts will most likely just follow a gradual path until that point with no real noteable price action so if you buy now you'll need to be patient.

Fud is extremely high and nobody is buying alts. Next run is likely to be alt centric.

Early replies (first 3)

61923244
>>61923233
$1 doge?

61923248
>>61923233
ive been on this godforsaken board for nearly 10y now, and on average, 1 out of 120 predictions seem to be close to 80%. so im gonna go out on a limb and call bs because you're just hopeposting for your own peace of mind, like most do

61923254
>>61923248
Then you should know when /biz/ is screaming alts are dead and it's the end of the world you should be buying (now).

All bottom indiciators are signaling I can't help you any more than that.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61923404
>>61923254
you're not helping anyone. Imo the most true prediction is always the one that causes the most pain. I'm gonna assume we get a bulltrap with the clarity act to fuck over everyone and then total capitulation by end of summer, and then we get rigged green candles

61923412
>>61923254
>>61923404
like, they frontran the cycle, making marketsentiment think this is it, no more cycles just upwards. Then FUCKED everyone. Then we got the small pump in end of 2025, fucking over everyone again that went in there thinking NOW its the time. Now, they crashed the market and everyone on le reddit and 4chan perpetuated that, oh, actually the cycles are still perfect, complete with an anon posting a year back and calling the top based on cycles.
Now the whole market thinks we're in bearmarket with a glimmer of hope (clarity act), so when that delivers, and only gives a small pump, then imo we're at absolute demoralization. Because the only people bagholding right now are the ones that think Clarity Act is gonna do something, which are the utility and schizo coins (stellar, cardano, XRP, link)

61923443
>>61923412
not buying your underwater shitcoin bags, sorry

61923453
>>61923443
kek ESL

61923457
>>61923233
>>61923244
>>61923248
i want to buy XRP or XLM, but im afraid the price is gonna drop so far that I won't know the best time to buy

there will be a rebound when people start hating bitcoin for all the reasons they should have over a decade ago

61923459
>>61923453
frega un cazzo del tuo inglese di merda coglione ebreo

61923496
>doing an XRP
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

61923506
>>61923412
You've just repeated what i've said though. Yes there'll likely be a bull trap this year but it will still offer much higher prices that now. Not that we won't return to similar prices by the end of the year but I just don't think alts set new lows. Bitcoin, probably.

>>61923457
XRP is imo a risky buy as we've no precedent how it might perform from now on if what i've said plays out. I can see 10-12 usd in the next run.

61923509
Alts are actually going to 0. Don't buy alts
>t. oldfag that has seen this rodeo before

61923525
theres zero reason to believe alts will go up again ever. bitcoin went from 15k to 125k and they barely moved, the only notable one was xrp

its very obvious the risk vs reward just isnt there for alts anymore. any of them can fall another 90% and at best you're looking at a 3-5x if you enter the exact bottom

61923536
alts went up because it was the best casino. prediction markets sports betting are better casinos now. binary yes/no outcomes are much easier to stomach than the never ending list of trash that makes alts go to zero

61923781
>>61923254
Truth. I'll rely on greed to return.

61925137
>>61923525
>theres zero reason to believe alts will go up again ever
Exactly the mentality i'm looking for to mark bottoms. Buying bottoms is never easy and it always feels like you're being stupid buying at bottoms.

61926573
>>61923244
Checked
$1+ doge on april 4th is the signal lads
That's when all heaven breaks loose

61926589
>>61923233
Not buying your heavy bags kike

61926661
>>61926573
>mt gox id
it's over

61926664
>>61923506
>10-12 usd xrp

all opinions discarded. this was the bottom. crypto will go up, stutter, and rocket. why you may ask? because im a 6th density seer and you're all retarded coomer animals.

>5/5 on premonitions since last summer

61926738
>>61923233
This but unironically

61928293
>>61923506
So buy bitcoin then xrp as they’re the only two that matter

61928304
I'd be happy to at least break even. I bought Arbitrum on 10/10 and only lost money since then.

item.title: Today is the day — 30 replies — 13.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Today is the day

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-03-03 08:31 UTC

OP

Lunar eclipse is today anons. Last solar eclipse Feb 17th was the local bottom. Full moon/lunar eclipse historically marks the crash day according to Puetz cycle.

BTC is going to crash. A lot of blood. Mercury retrograde just started, we're in the crash window until March 9th. Elliott Wave confirms 3rd of 3rd wave down on SPX which drags everything with it.

And now US/Israel vs Iran is actually popping off. Late Saturday night attack and counter-attack.

Every piece is falling into place at the same time — the astro, the technicals, the geopolitics. This isn't a coincidence.
Alts are getting absolutely destroyed. This isn't random volatility.

DYOR. Not financial advice.

Early replies (first 3)

61923231
>>61923226
Bring me 1500 eth or stfu already pls! Thank you for your attention to this matter!

61923232
>>61923226
Nice Schizo rant

61923238
>>61923231
>>61923232
You can pump this in a matter of hours. Tonight will be a day to remember. Everything has brought us to today. I just want to help all those perma bulls out there and their longs. The ones that listen to shitty youtubers and crypto influencers like Raol Paul, the granny from Ark Investors, etc.

I like /biz/ and just wanna help.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61923274
>>61923238
We will see i approtiate your opinion but i dont believe in it.

61923329
Okay bobo

Just shorted 100x on chinkbase

61923339
>>61923329
I'm not a bear. I hold and stack 2 crypto assets. I'm shorting the rest. I'm just telling you because even if /biz/ has become a very slow board, I had a lot of fun with all of you in the past.

61923345
>>61923274
>approtiate

I also don’t believe your opinion, Rakesh

61923357
>>61923226
Thanks brother, but we know
This will be the bottom of the cycle, it began in early 2025
Next blood moon will be seen in 2028, every bull cycle came after a 12 to 18 months long blood moon period

61923389
>>61923345
Its european hours amerimutt.

61924238
>>61923226
Well who could have thought

61924257
>>61923226
>Mercury retrograde
That's some dumb woman shit right there

61924557
Oh well I guess op went silent and is looking for the next schizo date

61924669
>>61923226
>Lunar eclipse is today anons.
Tomorrow

61925218
It's all coming together. 33333

61926891
Op?

61926971
>>61923226
well this thread aged gracefully like all schizo threads do

>>61923238
>crypto influencers like Raol Paul
he seems to be right about the macro impuls turning here now doesnt he, while everyone else is calling for the spx to be bottomless

61926981
>>61923238
>b-but i come to you out of concern!

61927049
>>61923226

61927075
Are you ready for the purim human sacrifice for moshiach, /biz/?

61927077
>>61923226
Astrology is astrology for men.

61927085
>>61923226
Everytime I see OP's picrelated I can't help but think of my neighbor who looks exactly the same and makes the same face whenever she sees me. It's a combination of mostly concern, a bit of disgust and contempt, a touch of latent sexual attraction, and a sprinkle of empathy

61927272
>>61923226
"Blood" can work both ways. Shorters might just get super rekt here. Bottom is likely in, and it's just waiting for people to realize it.

61927302
I'm hodling, I'm not getting rekt by some moon magic

61927751
op here. It already begun. Just as predicted. 333

61927768
2.5% drop ain't shit. Show us some real blood.

61927780
Just woke up and I was phoneposting from bed >>61927751

61927841
Tell me more about this annular eclipse on Feb.17th

61927843
Mercury is in retrograde

61927978
>>61923226
Link bullrun will finally start. TONIGHT!

61928200
OP here. I told you. It's gonna be painful for those not shorting.
